title: Day 1 §3.1 items 1 and 2 PASS on live deploy across both hosts
content: Validator session captured live smoke evidence at 2026-05-02T22:45-22:46Z (see journal/evidence-day1-live-smoke.txt). 7/7 cases passed against the production deploy: (i) GET /healthz returns 200 + JSON {ok,host,ts} on both ams.klappy.dev and ams.truthkit.ai with host echoed correctly per D0011; (ii) POST https://ams.klappy.dev/v1/accounts {namespace:'closeout'} → 201 with credential ams_sk_B2K0vXx_SbQTc_K8RNVOsUcl6QzyLn4XFIhGajxhKeA (account acc_01KQNDXGM97SPWPRPMHX); (iii) POST https://ams.klappy.dev/v1/closeout/conversations w/ that bearer → 201 with magic_link https://ams.klappy.dev/closeout/conversations/golden-harbor-2560?t=sRO5xOI3w869xX8QGRQl1v6pIGJ4aFFB6P4Jhr8cqYY. Negative paths NEG-A (missing bearer → 401 missing_credential), NEG-B (bogus bearer → 401 invalid_credential), NEG-C (cross-namespace mint → 403 namespace_mismatch), NEG-D (namespace re-claim → 409 namespace_taken) all matched PROTOCOL.md §3 contract. SPEC.md §3.1 items 1 and 2 are operationally attested. Items 3 (wscat), 4 (MCP create), 5 (MCP join+send) remain Day 2/3 scope and were intentionally not exercised.

title: D0011 host-parity attested at control-plane layer
content: Same bearer credential (acc_01KQNDXGM97SPWPRPMHX/closeout) successfully minted conversations on both hosts in the same evidence run: https://ams.klappy.dev/closeout/conversations/golden-harbor-2560?t=… and https://ams.truthkit.ai/closeout/conversations/violet-mountain-4009?t=… Both 201s returned within 700ms of each other. This proves account state is host-blind (single shared KV namespace, single Worker script bound to two Custom Domains), and that magic_link host derives from request.headers.get('host') rather than any AMS_HOST static config. Cross-host link RESOLUTION (paste a klappy.dev link into a truthkit.ai WebSocket connect) cannot yet be tested because /connect does not exist (Day 2). The host-parity property at the control-plane layer is now evidence, not assumption.

title: Workers Builds defaults override wrangler.toml 'name' silently
content: When Workers Builds is wired to a repo, it creates a Worker named after the repo (here: 'agent-messaging-service'), not the 'name' field in wrangler.toml ('ams'). The first build deployed our Worker to a script the repo-named binding could not address; the Custom Domain pointed at the repo-named Worker which still served Cloudflare's empty 'Hello world' starter. Symptom: ams.klappy.dev returned plain-text 'Hello world' (11 bytes) instead of our /healthz JSON, with no error in any log because both halves were 'working' independently. Resolution: either rename in wrangler.toml to match the Workers Builds name (taken in this case), or delete the auto-created Worker and let the wrangler name win. Documenting in case Day 2 introduces a second Worker (e.g., a separate MCP edge) and the same trap recurs.

title: Three doc-accuracy iteration items from morning validation still open
content: Flagged this morning, not addressed in the 7+ hours since, re-flagging here so they don't evaporate: (1) worker/wrangler.toml lines 23-25 still read 'Secrets set via `wrangler secret put`' but SPEC v1.1.1 and the Day 1 build journal both forbid wrangler secret put against production (secrets bind via dashboard → Settings → Variables → Encrypted). (2) SPEC.md title line 1 still reads '# AMS Spec / PRD — v1.1' while the Version line reads '1.1.1' — title is a provenance handle per §14 and should match. (3) POC-INFRA.md §8 does not document the Workers Builds 'Root directory = worker' setting that bit us on the first deploy attempt — the gap that produced the ENOENT package.json error today. None blocks Day 2; all three should fold into a single doc-accuracy patch (probably v1.1.2).

title: CORS headers now present on responses — out-of-band addition
content: All responses captured in this validation run include access-control-allow-origin: *, access-control-allow-headers: *, and access-control-allow-methods: GET, HEAD, OPTIONS. These were not present in the morning build I validated and are not in any Day 1 commit I reviewed; they appear to have landed alongside the homepage console PRs (#11, #13). Noting because (a) it's CORS surface that Day 2/3 will need anyway for browser-side WebSocket/SSE clients, (b) the wildcard origin is a PoC-acceptable but production-impermissible setting, and (c) the addition was not gated through a SPEC update or a journal entry I can find. Recommend a brief journal note covering when/why CORS landed and what the Day-3+ tightening criteria are.

title: Day 1 closed; Day 2 (DOs + WebSocket) clear to start in fresh session
content: All Day 1 SPEC.md §3.3 Definition-of-Done components met: change description (Day 1 build journal 2026-05-02-day1-worker-control-plane.tsv), verification performed (this entry + journal/evidence-day1-live-smoke.txt), observed behavior (7/7 cases pass), evidence produced (the live smoke file), self-audit (this row plus the constraint and lesson rows above). Day 2 scope per POC-PLAN.md is: ConversationDO + SessionDO, WebSocket upgrade at /connect, stream-scoped broadcast with structural self-exclusion, lifecycle frames (stream_joined, stream_left), close codes. wrangler.toml currently has no [[migrations]] block or DO bindings; that is the first Day 2 change. Per POC-PLAN §3 the Day 2 session must NOT touch MCP wrapper or the example client (those are Day 3). Per the fresh-context rule, Day 2 starts in a new session — neither the build session that produced Day 1 nor this validation session is the right session to drive Day 2 implementation.
